The Official BNI Podcast

A weekly audio discussion with Dr. Ivan Misner, the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, the world's largest business networking organization.

Episode 590: I’m Really Busy

January 9, 2019 by Ivan Misner 18 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode

Synopsis

Today’s theme is activity vs. results, the conversation Dr. Misner has had since he started BNI 34 years ago this week.

Meaghan Chitwood, Director of Global Training for BNI explains that your activities have to change as you become busier, because you need to get more results from the same amount of time.

An example of this is conducting one-to-ones. During your first one-to-one with a member, you’ll probably have a more general discussion to get to know each other. But on subsequent one-to-ones, share the specific things you’re working on and the referrals you need.

If you’re doing something that works for everyone else and it’s not working for you, ask yourself “How can I do this differently and get a better result?”

Take a look at the tasks you’re engaging in—both within and outside of BNI—and eliminate the tasks that aren’t getting results. If you think BNI is taking too much time, talk to your director consultant about how to work your BNI program more effectively.

If there’s a skill you need to hone in BNI, search BNI® Business Builder for that topic and you’ll find tons of resources.

Complete Transcript of Episode 590

Priscilla:
Hello everybody and welcome to the Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice and I’m coming to you from Live Oak Recording Studio from Berkeley, CA. And I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner.Hello Ivan. How are you today?

Ivan:
I am doing fantastic, Priscilla Thank you very much. This is the 34 anniversary of BNI. 34 years this week.

Priscilla:
Congratulations. Ivan: I don’t feel that old, Priscilla. You know?

Priscilla: I know.

Ivan:
I know, I know. I had somebody a few years ago say, they came up to me and they said it is really nice to be a part of an organization where the Founder is still alive.

Priscilla:
Oh wow.

Ivan:
Ok, I am still alive. Yeah, I hope to be around for a long time. It’s incredible that we are 34 years old this year and I am really excited to have as a guest of the podcast this week somebody who has been involved in BNI for a long time. I have with us Meaghan Chitwood.

Meaghan became a member of BNI in 2003. She became an Executive Director in 2004 so she was on the fast track. She is in the northern Alabama area and she joined the Global Support Team as the Director of Global Training in 2016. Her roots in the BNI world are so deep that it would be hard to get rid of her if we wanted to. That was in her bio, and I must say, Meaghan, we don’t want to get rid of you. It is awesome to have you on this podcast. Welcome.

Meaghan:
Thank you so much. Thank you so much.

Ivan:
So let’s talk about this topic this is really a great topic. Talk about that you are really talking about in many ways activity versus results. Talk about that concept and what you have in mind here because I think from Day 1 when I started BNI in 1985 the conversation I had was activity versus results. That is really what your theme is.

Meaghan:
Yes, absolutely and I think many people who know me personally and also if you listen to my bio, I am really busy is something that people say about me all the time and I actually don’t really like saying that ever. I am as busy as I want to be. That’s the way I look at tit. It is activity versus results. It’s figuring out are my activities getting me the ultimate results that I want?

When we all joined BNI, it was a while ago but I do remember it. We were joining BNI to become busier. We wanted more work. We wanted more referrals. What we tend to see if you have looked into your chapter lately, every once in a while, you will have somebody stand up and say, “I am too busy.” Somehow we have overshot the right amount of busy-ness.

I want to just give some tips on how you can rethink that and try and look at things because your activities have to change as you become busier because you have to figure out a way to get more results out of a shorter amount of time in essence.

Ivan:
Can I just say I think you are right on the mark, there. We all have a certain number of hours in the day. It is how we apply that. If you are an entrepreneur and you are busy and are becoming busier, you have to do something crazy like hire people, delegate or do things to have other individuals step in and assist because we are all busy. The question is are we productive? That’s the question here.

Meaghan:
Yes. Absolutely. I use this metaphor. My children and I and my family like to be on the lake. You like to be on the lake as well. And I think of it as being on a paddleboard. Imagine being on the paddleboard in the middle of the lake safely with no boats coming. Safely in the middle of the lake. You close your eyes and you start paddling in the direction that you think you are paddling and then the wind is blowing and there is a little bit of a current and maybe a boat comes by. I am still paddling exactly the way I was paddling but I can end up going absolutely in an opposite direction if I am not paying attention to my activity. If I continue to do the same activity, I am going to get knocked over at some point. I found myself saying I need to squeeze a second out of every minute of every day so that I can get more things done. It is just that matter of changing the activity or changing the quality of the activity that you are going to do. I would love to share some examples of how you can do that with BNI activities.

Ivan:
Please do that because that is key for members. Drill down.

Meaghan:
Let me give you a couple of examples. When you first meet somebody for the first time and you are just getting to know someone, you really have to give them time and you kind of have to see where the conversation goes. You are trying to figure out do we have things in common? That first 1-2-1 tends to be one that is a little more meandering in the tell me about your kids, tell me about your this, tell me about your that. It is a little more meandering and you just kind of have to five it a little bit more time.

But fast forward. If you know that person really, really well, your 1-2-1s can literally be like 15-30 minutes. “Here are the deals that I am working on right now. Do you know anybody who you could help connect me to?” The 1-2-1 is the same in activity but they look very very different because I already know you. You already know me. We already hang out. Right now is not for hanging out. It is for us to quickly get some strategic business done. This is just one example of how you can change the way that you do the activity to get the results.

Another one I want to share which is a similar route like 1-2-1, is doing a lot of 1-2-1’s and you’re not getting referrals from the 1-2-1’s, then you might want to look at how you are doing the 1-2-1. Because you can walk in- and again kind of like my last example walk in with a list of 10 people and you might be able to introduce me to five of them if we already know each other really well. Whereas, if you’re doing a bunch of 1-2-1’s for people and you’re not resulting in anything, you need to look at it and say, “Okay, what do I need to do differently to get to the end result of the referral?” Which is why we do the 1-2-1’s in the first place! You got to just look at it and just say, “Okay what can I do differently to make this really work?” And like I said in the very beginning, we all joined BNI to get more business. We have to figure how to catch that business when it comes to play. Sometimes it is basic time management skills, “Okay, I’m going to manage this better and I’m going to make phone calls at this time of the day because I’ve got to be able to return all these referrals that I’m getting.” I got to think through how to use my time management to really make sure that I’m on top of my game on a regular basis. 

Ivan:

Time management skills are key. I have a person that I know that talked about being busy and never had time to do whatever and they were a coffee barista. Like really dude? You don’t have time? You make coffee for a living!  It’s how we use that time that is key. I meet BNI members that say, “I don’t have time to do this or that.” But how you manage that time is the key, and I think that is what you’re talking about here.

Meaghan:

Absolutely! Let me give you can example: task lists. I have a task list but what I found what was happening about two weeks ago was I putting more stuff on the task list than what was coming off. It was more like I was spending all day long creating a task list that I need to do, and no time in the day for doing the things on the task list. But you know what I could have said? I could have said that task lists don’t work. But task lists work for every other person on the planet- except me! For some reason it just wasn’t working for me. So I had to look at it and say, “How can I do this differently and get a better result?” Luckily, I asked the right person the right question and he said, “Put the task on your calendar for however long you think it is going to take you .” And I’m telling you, since then, I’ve had the most productive last few weeks. But I mean, I’ve been doing this for 15 years, you would think that I would have been able to come up with that on my own. But I didn’t. I have to spend the time to sit back and say, “Okay what is working about this? What activities am I doing? Oh, these activities aren’t working. What do I take off my plate and equates do I put on my plate? How do I do this differently so that I can really make it work going forward?” We want BNI members to be in BNI for life! But it kills me every time I hear someone say, “Oh, I’m too busy and I can’t do BNI anymore.” And that makes me think if you are doing the activity to get the result you need?

Ivan:

That is true. Sometimes, though, when people say that they don’t have time for BNI, there are other issues. But let’s not assume that’s the case here. I think it is a time management issue, for sure. One of the things I like to do for time management, that I have said on my podcast before, is I color-code everything in my calendar and it’s related to the things I should be doing verses the things that I have to do. And I color-code in my calendar the things that I really want to to be doing or should be doing all in green. The things that drive forward my business to my role are in green. Everything else is in different colors. And by the way Meaghan red is bad, very bad. Usually red has lawyers involved. 

Meaghan:

Red has such a bad rep these days.

Ivan:

Yeah, well there’s a good reason. Anyways, it is about affective time management and using your time wisely. So I would recommend the directors and/or members of BNI to color-code their calendars for things they really should be doing- and  marketing is one of the things they should be doing.

Meaghan:

Absolutely. Also, if you do feel like BNI is taking too much time, reach out and talk to somebody about it because many of our Director consultants are available to help you figure out what you’re doing affectively and what you aren’t doing affectively. We don’t want BNI to take up a lot of time. It doesn’t have to, but you do have to work it. But you don’t have to work it 40 hours a week. So we want to help you find that balance and help you nail down what you can do, like I said, to squeeze every second you can out of the activities that you are doing.

Ivan:

Well, we need to wrap up. There are a number of things that people can do to really learn how to be more affective in BNI, and one of them is a project that you head up, and that is BNI University. I love BNI University! Meaghan, I want to put on record just saying that this is the formation of everything I had hoped we would be able to do in BNI- in terms of educating people in how to network more affectively. That’s one of the things I think people should spend a little time on, because it is sharpening their axe, you want to talk about BNI University very briefly? 

Meaghan:

Yes. So for anything that you do in BNI and you need help on how to hone that skill and do it more affectively, go into BNI University and just search for that topic. If it is referrals, it’s 1-2-1’s. There stuff on that that topic, and a bunch of other things you can find. There’s a bunch of great resources there!

Ivan:

There really is! And if you love BNI University, Meaghan made that happen. If you don’t like it, well, it’s somebody else’s… 

Meaghan really made BNI University happen. And I am really impressed with that program. I know how much work you have done on that, and I’m really impressed, Meaghan. I love it! I recommend everyone to go to that. It is one for the ways to make your time really effective because you’re sharpening your axe.

Anything you want to say before we wrap up here, Meaghan?

Meaghan:

I will also make sure that there is a resource available to you on BNI University to help you with some ideas on how you can look at what you’re doing, and make sure her activities that you are doing are getting the results that you want as well. So look for that on BNI University.

Thank you so much for having me, I really appreciate it. I feel so special being here on the 34th anniversary of BNI. I want to sincerely congratulate you for creating this amazing organization that all of us get to take a part in.

Ivan:

Thank you, Meaghan. I appreciate it. I got to tell you, I love this business. I love this organization. I love what I do. I’m happy to be here, and I’m happy to have you here on out 34th anniversary. 

Back to you, Priscilla.

Priscilla:

Okay, well that is it for this week. Thank you both so much. We are never too busy to hear such great information.

For more of Ivan’s content, go to IvanMisner.com. Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Special Guest Meaghan Chitwood Posted in Getting The Most From BNI, Special Guests Tagged with BNI® Business Builder

    January 11, 2019 at 10:35 pm

    Happy 34th Anniversary! Activity vs Results is something everyone needs to work harder at from time to time. Thanks for giving some suggestions on how to do that

    Reply

    • Vinayak Bhatkalkar says

      September 23, 2019 at 5:32 pm

      The more 121 activity you do the more business can be generated

  2. Marilyn Brennan says

    January 14, 2019 at 12:58 pm

    Good advice for 1 2 1’s!

    Reply

  3. Jay Johnson says

    January 14, 2019 at 7:52 pm

    Happy Anniversary to BNI. Great episode. I too color code my calendar as a means of prioritizing what’s really important. Somethings are simply non-negotiable for me and color coding the activities on my schedule help me remember that.

    Reply

    • Vinayak Bhatkalkar says

      September 23, 2019 at 5:45 pm

      Yes, it is true. This scrutinize your schedule.
      Help you plan your day to day activities.
      You remember exactly what to do.
      Your work is streamlined.

      Reply

  4. Chip Galusha says

    January 15, 2019 at 12:19 pm

    This episode brings to mind one of my favorite Lao Tzu quotes: “Time is a created thing. To say I don’t have time is to say I don’t want to.”

    Reply

  5. Paul says

    July 11, 2019 at 8:45 am

    This is a great podcast especially how to get more results in the same time.
    The sound quality in some sections was not good however.

    Reply

    • Vinayak Bhatkalkar says

      September 23, 2019 at 6:00 pm

      Yes,you learn more thing by listening to podcast.This for inturns helps to grow more business.
      Sound quality depends upon the network by your respective mobile operators.
      By listening to podcasts you have flexibility in time & at same time you can learn business by our own founder Ivan Misner

      Reply

  6. Sarah Karingi says

    November 7, 2019 at 7:27 am

    We always create time for people or things we value. Saying we are too busy for 121s only means we don’t value the very people who are busy looking for referrals for us

    Reply

  7. Jeffrey Osborne says

    September 24, 2020 at 12:16 am

    This topic is so important. I am a great user of David Allen’s GTD system which hee we los me manage all BNI activities

    Reply

  8. Dan Glazier says

    September 30, 2020 at 5:41 am

    Amazing podcast. Thank you Ivan and Meagan!

  9. Oanh Henry says

    October 19, 2020 at 10:37 pm

    I also love BNI University! Yes, great message.

    Reply

  10. Marilyn Pasana says

    November 15, 2020 at 6:12 am

    Learned so much from this podcast . Thanks Meagan and Ivan . Happy anniversary BNI

    Reply

  11. Paula Cluver says

    March 23, 2022 at 11:29 am

    I have only been in BNI for six months, so I like how she hit on “first 1-2-1’s.” It was awkward at first meeting with everyone to the point that I did not want to do anymore. My recent six month evaluation, my mentor helped me understand how to make them easier and this podcast really hit on this problem.

    Reply

  12. Gina M Moreno says

    February 22, 2024 at 4:34 pm

    Congratulations on 34 years! Great podcast. Its great to learn from the best!

    Reply

  13. Josh Iverson says

    August 14, 2024 at 7:03 am

    Loved this! Every day as business owners we are going to do some thing that’s “well above us”and “well below us”. Love to be a guest in the podcast.

    Reply

  14. cynthia Uy Kieng says

    February 16, 2025 at 7:48 pm

    Good Advice need to set our schedule properly too.

    Reply

  15. EMMANUEL T says

    April 24, 2026 at 3:19 am

    Yes we aren`t that busy its about prioritising our tasks and work.
    it was indeed an eye opener

    Thanks Meghan and Ivan for the insights
